title: Refactor bad_guards.ex preserving compile-legal guard expressions
prompt: Refactor ALL functions in test_fixtures/bad_guards.ex. Every function must use guards that would actually compile. Specifically: `validate_age/1` uses guard `when is_integer(age) and age >= 0 and age <= 150`. `validate_email/1` keeps `String.contains?` in the body because it's not allowed in guards. `call_service/1` uses `when is_binary(req)` (not `not is_nil`). `process/1` uses an assertive type guard. `format_user/1` uses `when is_binary(name) and byte_size(name) > 0`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Validator do ... end`.

must_not_contain: when is_integer(age) and age >= 0 and age <= 150 and String.contains?

tier_rationale: Requires knowing the guard restriction subset AND applying Credo rules consistently across 5 functions.
